channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: What to know about the electrical grid failure that plunged Cuba into darkness
item.description: A few things to know after millions of people in Cuba were left without electricity for two days when the nation's electrical grid collapsed.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Rodriguez, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Netanyahu promises retaliation following assassination attempt by Hezbollah
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu warned Hezbollah that its alleged attempt to assassinate him and his wife was a "grave mistake.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Japan's governing party headquarters is attacked with firebombs and suspect is arrested
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — A man threw several firebombs into the headquarters of Japan’s governing party in Tokyo on Saturday, then crashed his car into the fencing of the prime minister's residence, police said. There were no reports of injuries.
item.dc:creator: YURI KAGEYAMA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Ukraine’s ‘victory plan’ receives mixed reactions from Western allies
item.description: President Volodymyr Zelenskyy’s plan to end Ukraine’s nearly three-year war with Russia is getting mixed reactions from Western allies.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Arhirova, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Video published by Ukraine allegedly shows North Korean soldiers in Russia
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — A video purporting to show dozens of North Korean recruits lining up to collect Russian military fatigues has been released by Ukrainian officials, who say it shows the introduction of troops sent by Pyongyang into the conflict.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Strike on Netanyahu home 'a signal' Iran proxies not incapacitated, expert says
item.description: A recent drone strike targeting the home of Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu is sending "a signal" that Iran-backed militant groups are not "incapacitated," despite losing senior leadership in recent weeks, according to a Middle East expert. Daniel Levy, the president of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Cuba gets some electricity back after major power outage left millions in the dark
item.description: Cuba’s government says some electricity has been restored on the island after one of the country’s major power plants failed and left millions without electricity for two days.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: The first migrants sent to Albania for processing return to Italy
item.description: SHENGJIN, Albania (AP) — An Italian navy ship on Saturday took back to Italy the first 12 migrants from newly opened asylum processing centers in Albania following a contentious court decision in Rome.
item.dc:creator: VLASOV SULAJ and LLAZAR SEMINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: North Korea says it discovered the remains of a South Korean drone in Pyongyang
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korea said Saturday that authorities found the remains of a South Korean drone during a search in the capital, Pyongyang, and that the drone proved that the South’s military was behind the alleged drone infiltrations over the city’s skies earlier in the month.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Guatemalan judge grants investigative journalist Zamora house arrest and his family celebrates
item.description: The family of a Guatemalan journalist has celebrated his transfer to house arrest following his jailing for over two years amid his newspaper’s anti-corruption investigations.
item.dc:creator: Sonia Perez D., Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Funeral home in Poland apologizes after a corpse falls out of a hearse in traffic
item.description: WARSAW, Poland (AP) — A funeral home in Poland issued an unusual apology on Saturday after a corpse that it was transporting fell out of a hearse and into traffic.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: A drone targets the Israeli prime minister's house during new barrages with Hezbollah
item.description: Israel’s government says a drone targeted Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu’s house on Saturday, with no casualties.
item.dc:creator: Adam Schreck And Samy Magdy, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Belgrade's landmark Hotel Yugoslavia faces likely demolition, with many opposed
item.description: BELGRADE, Serbia (AP) — Belgrade’s iconic Hotel Yugoslavia, once a symbol of progress in the former socialist state of Yugoslavia that broke apart in the 1990s and a favorite gathering place for local residents as well as world leaders, now stands in eerie silence awaiting its likely demolition.
item.dc:creator: DUSAN STOJANOVIC, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Freedom of expression threatened more seriously in Gaza than in any recent conflict, UN expert says
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — Freedom of expression has been threatened more seriously in Gaza than in any recent conflict, with journalists targeted in the war-torn territory and Palestinian supporters targeted in many countries, a United Nations expert said Friday. Irene Khan, the U.N.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cuba’s grid goes offline with massive blackout after a major power plant fails
item.description: HAVANA (AP) — Cuba’s electrical grid went down Friday after one of the island’s major power plants failed, a day after a massive blackout swept across the Caribbean island and with no official estimate for when service will be restored.
item.dc:creator: ANDREA RODRÍGUEZ, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: UN expands arms embargo on Haiti to all types of arms and ammunition
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The U.N. Security Council voted unanimously Friday to expand the arms embargo in Haiti to all types of weapons and ammunition, expressing grave concern at the extremely high levels of gang violence and criminal activities in the impoverished Caribbean nation.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korean intelligence says North has sent troops to aid Russia’s war in Ukraine
item.description: South Korea's spy agency said North Korea has sent troops to support Russia's war in Ukraine, transferring 1,500 North Korean special operation forces to the Russian city of Vladivostok.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cuba's power grid fails, plunging country into darkness
item.description: Cuba's government closed schools and non-essential industries and sent most state workers home in a last-ditch effort to keep the lights on for residents.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: IDF video: Israeli tank fires at building where Hamas leader Yahya Sinwar was barricaded before being killed
item.description: A building where Hamas chief Yahya Sinwar had been barricaded was targeted by Israeli tank fire before the terror leader was killed on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gangs attack neighborhoods across Haiti's capital in new wave of violence
item.description: PORT-AU-PRINCE, Haiti (AP) — Multiple gangs attacked several communities in the Haitian capital of Port-au-Prince on Friday, setting fire to homes and a church as bullets whizzed through the area. At least one woman was killed as gangs opened fire in Solino, St.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Whelan says Brittney Griner was 'great help' in advocating for his freedom
item.description: Former U.S. Marine Paul Whelan said WNBA star Brittney Griner was of “great help” in advocating for his freedom after being detained in Russia since 2018.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Ex-Indian government employee charged in US assassination plot
item.description: The Justice Department announced charges against a former Indian government employee in the murder plot against dual U.S.-Canadian citizen Gurpatwant Singh Pannun, a prominent Sikh separatist.
item.dc:creator: Sarakshi Rai, The Hill

item.title: Hamas official confirms Yahya Sinwar's death
item.description: A senior Hamas official confirmed Friday the militant group's leader Yahya Sinwar was killed by the Israeli military. Khalil al-Hayya mourned the killing of Sinwar, the orchestrator of the Oct.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: South Korean intelligence says North has sent troops to aid Russia's war in Ukraine
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea's spy agency said Friday that North Korea has dispatched troops to support Russia's war against Ukraine. If confirmed, the move would bring a third country into the war and intensify a standoff between North Korea and the West.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rights groups urge Thailand to release Vietnamese activist, saying he faces torture if extradited
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — A group of nearly three dozen rights groups called on Thailand's prime minister Friday to release a Vietnamese activist who has been ordered extradited home to face imprisonment on terrorism charges, saying he faces the possibility of torture if returned.
item.dc:creator: DAVID RISING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Military voters see 'direct attack' from GOP election challenges
item.description: Republicans are coming under fire from veterans and military groups for attempting to restrict the votes of overseas service members through a series of lawsuits in battleground states.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Israel says Hamas' top leader Yahya Sinwar was killed in Gaza
item.description: Israel’s foreign minister has confirmed that Israeli troops in Gaza have killed Hamas’ top leader Yahya Sinwar, a chief architect of last year's attack on Israel that sparked the war.
item.dc:creator: Wafaa Shurafa, Samy Magdy, Jack Jeffery, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Zelenskyy outlines his 'victory plan' to Ukraine's lawmakers, including a call to join NATO
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — President Volodymyr Zelenskyy told lawmakers Wednesday that Ukraine’s Western partners are increasing pressure to negotiate with Russia, but he hinted such talks would be unfavorable to Kyiv as he unveiled what he called his “victory plan” for the war.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB and HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: A look at the oldest stone tools ever discovered, dating back 3.3M years
item.description: The oldest stone tools ever discovered were unearthed in Kenya by a group of scientists at the Lomekwi 3 site. The stone tools unearthed are simple in their design and pre-date humans.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Maybe advanced civilizations aren’t all that advanced: Scientist
item.description: Antonio Gelis-Filho writes that perhaps advanced civilizations have reached the limits of their technology, and we may be close to doing the same.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation
